title: Inspect and live-edit 3D WebGL elements
summary: Google demonstrated the HTML-in-Canvas API, which allows developers to inspect and live-edit 3D WebGL elements while maintaining UI accessibility and searchability. The API enables use of standard development tools for WebGL-based interfaces.
writeup: This video from Google for Developers covers the HTML-in-Canvas API, which lets developers inspect and live-edit 3D WebGL elements. It matters because UI rendered inside a canvas has traditionally been opaque to standard tooling. The key takeaway: the API keeps interface content accessible, searchable, and inspectable using standard developer tools while it sits alongside WebGL graphics.
